evidence.scientific_clarity: Six KDE ridges clearly stacked with overlap, each cluster labelled; x-axis label and distribution shapes are immediately readable at single-column width.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' both set; Arial prioritised over DejaVu Sans; axis tick labels 7 pt, x-label 8 pt, cluster labels 7 pt bold — all consistent with Nature style.
evidence.palette: Sequential cool-to-warm palette is perceptually ordered and avoids pure red/green traffic-light coding, but colourblind safety is partial: deep blue and teal (clusters 1-3) may be hard to distinguish for deuteranopes; no explicit CVD check tool used.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout used, 3.5×3.2 in single-column format, left labels offset outside plot area, no overlapping text, y-range includes 15 % padding below and 115 % above top KDE — well-utilised space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: No legend needed (labels on ridges); caption matches figure content; ordering bottom-to-top stated in caption and matches plot; no extraneous whitespace or crowding visible.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains CID TrueType fonts (Arial-BoldMT, ArialMT) embedded and subsetted — confirms pdf.fonttype=42 worked; text is selectable in PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial; brief asks for figure.pdf + figure.png + caption.tex + gen_figure.py — all present, SVG is a bonus; minor: brief did not request SVG so it is fine but superfluous.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names plot type, kernel, bandwidth, sample size range (n≈400-500), ordering direction, colour progression, and data source — provides specific numerical claim and describes what the reader sees.

overall_strengths: Exemplary typography: pdf.fonttype=42 confirmed via pdffonts, Arial used throughout, consistent 7-8 pt sizing suitable for single-column Nature format.
Caption is thorough and specific, citing kernel method, bandwidth choice, sample size, and ordering convention — reduces reviewer queries substantially.
overall_weaknesses: Palette is not fully colourblind-safe: the blue-to-teal transition (clusters 1-3) poses a challenge for deuteranopia/protanopia; a CVD-tested palette (e.g. viridis or a Okabe-Ito-derived sequential ramp) would improve accessibility.
figure.svg is produced but not requested in the brief; harmless, but script could be simplified by removing the SVG save call.
